name: arbhar
description: Granular processor with six 10-second buffers, two grain engines, 1V/oct pitch tracking, onboard capture and onset detection, and mono or stereo input paths. Built-in microphone and USB sample import turn it into a live recorder as well as an effect.

controls.name: Input Level
controls.description: Sets input gain before the limiter. Around 10 o'clock is unity; clockwise drives the preamp.
controls.detail: unity at ~10:00 · analogue limiting on high levels

controls.name: Dry/Wet
controls.description: Crossfades between the dry input and the granular engines.

controls.name: Output Level
controls.description: Sets the level at both outputs.

controls.name: Capture
controls.description: Starts and stops recording to the selected layer.
controls.detail: press to record · press again to stop

controls.name: Shift
controls.description: Accesses secondary functions such as capture modes, track-and-hold, and mode selection.
controls.detail: holds secondary state · used with Capture and Layer

controls.name: Layer
controls.description: Selects one of the six layers, or omega to link them through Scan.
controls.detail: alpha through zeta · omega = scan all layers

controls.name: Intensity
controls.description: Sets grain density and asynchronous versus synchronous grain generation.
controls.detail: centre = max grains · extremes disable continuous engine

controls.name: Strike
controls.description: Generates a single accent grain from the strike engine.
controls.detail: button and trigger input both fire a grain

controls.name: Grain Window
controls.description: Shapes the grain envelope from square to Gaussian to sawtooth.
controls.detail: CCW: square · noon: Gaussian · CW: descending saw

controls.name: Grain Direction
controls.description: Sets the probability of forward versus reverse playback.

controls.name: Length
controls.detail: ~4 ms to 3 s · center around 750 ms

controls.name: Scan
controls.description: Sets the playback position inside the active layer.
controls.detail: sets grain start position at grain onset

controls.name: Spray
controls.detail: CCW: tight cluster · CW: fully random layer access

controls.name: Pitch Deviation
controls.description: Adds per-grain random or quantized pitch offsets.
controls.detail: CCW: unquantized random · CW: quantized intervals

controls.name: Sensitivity
controls.description: Sets onset detector sensitivity or Input 2 level in stereo mode.

controls.name: Hold
controls.description: Sets the minimum time between onset detections or recorded events.

controls.name: Dub
controls.description: Sets sound-on-sound attenuation for overdub capture.
controls.detail: CCW: replace · noon: half-old/half-new · CW: full layering

inputs.name: Input
inputs.description: AC-coupled audio input for capture and playback processing.

inputs.name: Onset Input
inputs.description: Audio input for onset detection and stereo capture analysis.

inputs.name: Capture Pulse Input
inputs.description: Gate or trigger input that starts, stops, or retriggers capture.

inputs.name: Intensity CV Input
inputs.description: CV over grain density and amplitude distribution.

inputs.name: Strike Input
inputs.description: Trigger or gate input for the strike granular engine.

inputs.name: 1V/Octave Input
inputs.description: Pitch tracking input for grain transposition.

outputs.name: Output 1
outputs.description: Audio output. Grains are coin-tossed between both outputs by default.

outputs.name: Output 2
outputs.description: Second audio output. Unpatched, it normalizes to Output 1 for mono playback.

outputs.name: Pulse Output
outputs.description: Trigger or gate output from the onset engine.

behaviors.name: Two grain engines
behaviors.trigger: Intensity above minimum or Strike input
behaviors.description: The continuous engine runs from Length and Intensity, while the strike engine fires on Capture Button or Strike Input. Together they can generate up to 88 grains polyphonically.

behaviors.name: Layered capture
behaviors.description: arbhar records into one of six 10-second layers. Accumulative capture can pause and resume within a layer, turning one buffer into a composed collage.

behaviors.name: Scan and follow
behaviors.description: In omega, the Scan knob addresses all layers through position. In follow mode, the playback head tracks the record head, keeping grains anchored to captured motion.

behaviors.name: Onset-driven capture
behaviors.description: Onset analysis can arm capture and also generate triggers or gates from detected attacks. The Pulse Output becomes a usable event stream for external timing.

behaviors.name: Pitch deviation
behaviors.description: Counterclockwise adds random pitch drift. Clockwise adds quantized interval offsets, so each grain can move in a repeatable harmonic family instead of pure noise.

behaviors.name: Stereo and mono paths
behaviors.description: With only Output 1 patched, Output 2 normalizes to mono. Patching Output 2 opens stereo imaging and lets each side take independent grain distribution.

behaviors.name: Audio capture from the panel mic
behaviors.trigger: no cable at Input or Onset Input
behaviors.description: The onboard condenser microphone normalizes into the capture path. The module becomes a self-contained field recorder without external cabling.

patchIdeas.name: Frozen cloud
patchIdeas.patch: Layer alpha selected · Intensity near center · Length around 1/2 s · Scan around noon · Dry/Wet mostly wet · feed a slow pad into Input and let arbhar turn it into a stable frozen cloud with stereo spread.
patchIdeas.diagram:
arbhar:
* Layer: alpha
* Intensity: noon
* Length: ~1/2 s
* Scan: noon
* Dry/Wet: mostly wet

VCO:

Mixer:

- VCO (Out) -> arbhar (Input)
- arbhar (Output 1) -> Mixer (L)
- arbhar (Output 2) -> Mixer (R)

patchIdeas.name: Onset sampler
patchIdeas.patch: Onset Input listens to a drum loop · Capture Pulse Input stays unpatched · Hold set short · Sensitivity turned up · detected attacks trigger fresh grains and the Pulse Output can clock an external envelope for a synced stutter.
patchIdeas.diagram:
arbhar:
* Sensitivity: high
* Hold: short
* Capture: armed

Drum Loop:
Envelope:
Mixer:

- Drum Loop (Out) -> arbhar (Onset Input)
- Drum Loop (Out) -> arbhar (Input)
- arbhar (Pulse Output) t> Envelope (Trig)
- arbhar (Output 1) -> Mixer (L)
- arbhar (Output 2) -> Mixer (R)

patchIdeas.name: Wavetable sweep
patchIdeas.patch: Layer omega linked · Scan CV from a slow LFO · Grain Window around Gaussian · Grain Direction centered · Pitch Deviation slightly clockwise · the buffer becomes a moving wavetable that slides through all layers as the LFO rises and falls.
patchIdeas.diagram:
arbhar:
* Layer: omega
* Grain Window: noon
* Grain Direction: noon
* Pitch Deviation: slight CW

LFO:

Output:

- LFO (Out) >> arbhar (Scan CV Input)
- arbhar (Output 1) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Sound-on-sound smear
patchIdeas.patch: Dub set just past noon · Intensity at the low edge of continuous mode · Length long · Pitch at unity · repeated capture builds a smear of the original source while the dry path stays present under the wet grains.
patchIdeas.diagram:
arbhar:
* Dub: just past noon
* Intensity: low continuous
* Length: long
* Pitch: unity

VCO:
Output:

- VCO (Saw) -> arbhar (Input)
- arbhar (Output 1) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Pitch-shifted percussion
patchIdeas.patch: Strike engine active · Capture pulse from a sequencer gate · Pitch knob down an octave · Pitch Deviation turned up · each strike produces short pitched grains that sit on top of the dry rhythm as a pitched granular accent.
patchIdeas.diagram:
arbhar:
* Strike: ready
* Pitch: -1 octave
* Pitch Deviation: moderate CW

Sequencer:
Output:

- Sequencer (Gate) g> arbhar (Capture Pulse Input)
- Sequencer (Gate) t> arbhar (Strike Input)
- arbhar (Output 1) -> Output (In)
